auth.title: Sign in
auth.ssoRequired: This account uses organization SSO. Use the SSO button instead.

placeholder.description: This section will be connected to the control plane. Placeholder content only.

users.description: Local accounts registered in Kong Manager. Group membership drives Casbin roles.

error.forbidden: You do not have permission to view users (admin role required).
error.passwordMin: New password must be at least 8 characters, or leave empty to keep the current password.

users.editHint: Change username and/or set a new password (min. 8 characters). Role checkboxes replace the user’s groups when you save.
users.confirmDelete: Delete user "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
users.createHint: Password must be at least 8 characters. Usernames may include letters (any language), digits, _, ., @, -. Optionally assign roles before the first sign-in.
users.createError: Could not create user (duplicate name or invalid input).

auditLog.description: Append-only record of privileged actions (who did what, and when).
auditLog.empty: No audit entries yet. Actions such as creating users, updating roles, or deleting users are logged here.

error.forbidden: You do not have permission to view the audit log (admin role required).

rbac.description: Effective Casbin policies: which roles can access which paths, and which users belong to which roles.

error.forbidden: You do not have permission to view RBAC (admin role required).

roles.title: Roles
roles.policyHint: Path must start with / (e.g. /kong-admin/*) or be *. Action: GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, or *.
roles.deleteBlockedUsers: Remove all users from this role before deleting it.

clusters.description: Upstream Kong Admin URLs are stored per cluster (dynamic). The env KONG_ADMIN_URL only seeds the first "default" row when the table is empty; after that, add or edit clusters here. The UI calls the BFF at /kong-admin/c/{slug}/… and the BFF forwards to the matching admin_base_url.

error.forbidden: You do not have permission to manage clusters.

sso.title: SSO (OpenID Connect)
sso.description: Register each IdP with redirect URI {origin}{gui}/api/auth/oidc/{slug}/callback (append {gui} only when ADMIN_GUI_PATH is set). Set PUBLIC_BASE_URL on the server if the public origin is not inferred correctly.
sso.editLead: Update IdP settings. The redirect URI below must be added exactly in Keycloak (or your IdP) under the client’s Valid redirect URIs.
sso.redirectUriKeycloak: If Keycloak shows “Invalid parameter: redirect_uri”, the callback URL registered on the IdP does not match the URL Kong Manager sends. Copy the Redirect URI into the same Keycloak client as your Client ID (e.g. kong-manager-uat), under Valid redirect URIs — scheme, host, path, and port must match exactly.
sso.redirectUriSameHost: Use the same host in the browser as in Keycloak (localhost vs 127.0.0.1, or different ports, are different URIs). If FE is on port 8080 and BFF on 8081, the dev proxy must preserve the browser Host, or set server env PUBLIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8080 so the BFF builds redirect_uri correctly.
sso.createLead: After saving, register the Redirect URI below on your IdP for this client. Until it matches, login will fail with invalid redirect_uri.
sso.createValidation: Fill in URL slug, display name, issuer URL, client ID, and client secret.
sso.redirectPreviewNeedSlug: (Enter a URL slug to preview the redirect URI.)
sso.redirectUriLabel: Redirect URI (register this on the IdP)
sso.redirectUriRegister: Add this exact URL to Valid redirect URIs. You may use a trailing wildcard in Keycloak (e.g. https://host/*) only if it still matches the path your browser uses.
sso.secretPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep the current secret

error.forbidden: You do not have permission (admin role required).

global.error: An error has occurred, please try again

konnect-cta.name: Konnect
konnect-cta.description: The easiest way to get started with Kong Gateway.
konnect-cta.footer.description: No credit card required & free plan available
konnect-cta.feature.1: Simplify Gateway operations with Konnect's SaaS control plane
konnect-cta.feature.2: Test drive enterprise-grade plugins like OpenID Connect, mTLS, and OPA
konnect-cta.feature.3: Out of the box API Analytics
konnect-cta.feature.5: Deploy an API Dev Portal with registration management
